evals.prompt: I need a full prospect research brief on Amit Agarwal -- he's the VP of Engineering at Datadog. They reported strong Q4 earnings recently and keep expanding their platform beyond APM into security and CI/CD visibility. We sell developer infrastructure (CI/CD and deployment automation) and I want to figure out if he's the right entry point or if there's a better technical buyer on the platform engineering side. I'm planning outreach this week, maybe Thursday. We've never spoken to anyone at Datadog before, total cold. Our ICP is public or late-stage developer tooling and observability companies, 1000+ headcount, US-based. Can you do the full workup before my planning session tomorrow?
evals.expected_output: A complete prospect research brief on Amit Agarwal at Datadog covering all six phases: input collection confirmation, contact-level research (role, tenure, career trajectory, LinkedIn activity, signal interpretation), account-level research (business model, revenue from public filings, headcount, tech stack, market position, recent news including Q4 earnings and platform expansion), signal classification with Datadog's CI/CD expansion as a relevant signal, pain assessment connecting platform expansion pressure to CI/CD infrastructure needs, at least one specific hook tied to verifiable public information, product fit assessment, and recommended first touch with channel, tone, and goal.
expectations: To give the AE enough verified intelligence to write outreach that gets a reply, the research brief contains all required output sections with the mandatory header (TASK, ICP MATCH, CONFIGURATION, VERIFY DATA) — WHO IS section with career background sourced from LinkedIn or public profiles, WHAT IS DATADOG with real financials from public filings, WHAT IS HAPPENING NOW with classified signals, THE SPECIFIC PAIN, THE HOOK, PRODUCT FIT, and RECOMMENDED FIRST TOUCH

To surface timing signals that determine whether to reach out now or wait, signal classification categorizes Datadog's CI/CD platform expansion as a relevant signal with source attribution to earnings calls, press releases, or product announcements — and checks for additional signals from engineering job postings, recent acquisitions, or leadership changes that indicate active investment in the CI/CD space

To ensure the AE's outreach references something real and checkable, at least one hook is identified that is specific and verifiable — tied to a public event such as Datadog's earnings report, a product launch announcement, a specific job posting for CI/CD roles, or a conference talk — not a generic industry trend about observability platforms expanding

To qualify whether this deal is worth pursuing before the AE invests time, ICP match assessment evaluates Datadog against the stated criteria (public or late-stage, developer tooling / observability company, 1000+ headcount, US-based) and produces a STRONG / MODERATE / WEAK rating with specific reasoning — including whether selling CI/CD tooling to a company that builds CI/CD visibility creates a competitive tension that weakens fit

To connect Datadog's situation to a specific reason they would buy, the pain assessment constructs a causal chain from their platform expansion (moving beyond APM into security and CI/CD) to infrastructure scaling pressure — identifying whether Datadog is a buyer of CI/CD tooling for internal engineering needs or a competitor building CI/CD products, and how that distinction changes the sales approach

To determine who on the buying committee should receive the first message, the recommended first touch evaluates whether Amit Agarwal is the right entry point or whether a Director of Platform Engineering or Infrastructure lead would be more appropriate — specifying channel, tone, and goal with reasoning based on the prospect's seniority and Datadog's org structure

evals.prompt: I'm prepping for a warm intro to Meridian Financial Group -- the CRO Sarah Chen just got replaced about two months ago by a guy named Marcus Webb. He came from Stripe I think, or maybe one of the other payments companies. Meridian is a mid-market fintech doing B2B payments processing, they've been around maybe 8-9 years and I know they're north of $100M ARR because their CEO mentioned it on a podcast last year. We sell revenue intelligence software -- conversation analytics, deal inspection, forecast accuracy, that kind of thing. New CRO at a company that size almost always means the board wanted better pipeline visibility and forecast discipline. Our champion at Meridian was on Sarah's team and I'm worried he's going to get reorged out. Can you do the full research workup on Marcus Webb and the current state of Meridian so I know what I'm walking into? I need to figure out if our existing relationship survives this transition or if we're starting from scratch.
evals.expected_output: A complete prospect research brief on Marcus Webb at Meridian Financial Group covering the leadership transition as a central theme: contact-level research on Marcus Webb (career at Stripe or payments industry, time in role as new CRO, public activity, signal interpretation of a new executive in a revenue leadership seat), account-level research on Meridian (B2B payments model, revenue north of $100M ARR, headcount, tech stack, market position, recent news including the CRO change and any reorg signals), signal classification with new CRO as HOT, pain assessment connecting new revenue leadership to conversation analytics and forecast needs, hooks tied to the leadership change or Marcus's background, and recommended first touch that accounts for the existing relationship risk.
expectations: To protect an existing deal relationship at risk of reorg, the research brief identifies the CRO transition as the central strategic factor — WHO IS MARCUS WEBB includes career background (referencing the Stripe attribution from the AE's input, either verified with a source or explicitly flagged as user-provided and requiring verification), tenure calculated as approximately 2 months with signal interpretation that a new CRO at <6 months is a HOT receptivity window per the skill's signal criteria

To give the AE competitive intelligence on the account before the warm intro, WHAT IS MERIDIAN FINANCIAL GROUP covers real business model (B2B payments processing), revenue estimate referencing the CEO podcast mention (with any corroborating data if available, or explicitly flagged as single-source and unverified), headcount estimate with indication of confidence level, and market position — the company is NOT conflated with any other entity named Meridian

To identify whether this is a 'defend the deal' or 'expand the deal' situation, signal classification categorizes the CRO replacement as a HOT signal with source and date, and separately evaluates supporting signals — job postings for revenue operations or sales operations roles, reorg indicators, or changes in go-to-market strategy that reveal whether Marcus Webb is building a new team or inheriting the existing one

To arm the AE with a narrative that resonates with a new CRO's first-100-days priorities, the pain assessment constructs a specific causal chain: CRO replacement implies board-level dissatisfaction with revenue execution, which creates immediate demand for pipeline visibility, forecast accuracy, and deal inspection — connecting Marcus Webb's likely priorities directly to the revenue intelligence product category

To make the warm intro feel informed rather than generic, at least one hook is identified that is specific to Marcus Webb or the transition event — a post Marcus made about revenue methodology, an initiative he led at Stripe, or a Meridian job posting that signals his new priorities — not a generic 'new CROs need visibility' hook

To determine the right approach given the champion-at-risk situation, the recommended first touch addresses whether to lead through the existing champion or go direct to Marcus Webb — specifying channel and tone appropriate for a new C-level executive in his first 90 days, and flagging the reorg risk as a factor that affects timing and approach

evals.prompt: Just flagging a hot one -- I've been tracking Raj Patel, he's Director of Platform Engineering at Komodo Health. Over the last 3 weeks he's posted like 4 or 5 times on LinkedIn about observability challenges at scale, costs of running Datadog across hundreds of microservices, and how his team is evaluating alternatives. One post specifically mentioned wanting 'open-source-first observability that doesn't bankrupt the engineering budget' -- that was maybe 10 days ago and got like 200+ reactions. We sell an open-source observability platform (logs, metrics, traces) and this guy is literally describing our product in his posts. Komodo Health is a healthcare data analytics company, Series D or E, probably 500-800 people. I need the full research brief ASAP so I can reach out before he finishes his evaluation. This feels like a deal that's already in motion without us.
evals.expected_output: A complete prospect research brief on Raj Patel at Komodo Health with the LinkedIn activity as the primary HOT signal: contact-level research covering his role as Director of Platform Engineering, tenure, career trajectory, and detailed analysis of his recent LinkedIn posting pattern about observability challenges, account-level research on Komodo Health (healthcare data analytics model, funding stage, headcount, tech stack including current Datadog usage, market position), signal classification with his LinkedIn posts as HOT and any supporting signals, pain assessment connecting observability cost pressure at scale to the open-source observability platform, hooks drawn directly from his specific posts, and urgent recommended first touch given the active evaluation window.
expectations: To enter an active evaluation before the shortlist closes, the research brief treats urgency as a structural element — the WHO IS RAJ PATEL section catalogs his LinkedIn posting cadence (4-5 posts over 3 weeks), specific themes (Datadog cost at scale, microservices observability, open-source alternatives), engagement levels (200+ reactions on key post), and classifies this as a HOT signal per the skill's criteria (prospect posted about your problem area in last 14 days)

To surface whether Komodo Health has additional buying signals beyond one person's posts, the WHAT IS HAPPENING NOW section checks for corroborating signals — job postings for observability or platform engineering roles, recent funding that would fund infrastructure investments, tech stack signals from engineering job descriptions mentioning Datadog or alternatives — building a picture of whether this is one engineer's frustration or an organizational initiative

To give the AE a hook that demonstrates genuine awareness of Raj's perspective, at least one hook references a specific element from his LinkedIn activity — such as the 'open-source-first observability' theme — framed as referencing the theme of his public commentary rather than quoting his exact words, consistent with the skill's hook identification guidance

To qualify whether this account is worth fast-tracking, the ICP match assessment evaluates Komodo Health against reasonable criteria for an open-source observability vendor (healthcare data analytics company, late-stage funding, 500-800 headcount, engineering-heavy organization running microservices at scale) and produces a rating with reasoning about fit strength

To connect the prospect's stated pain to a specific buying rationale, the pain assessment builds a causal chain: hundreds of microservices generating high observability data volume, Datadog per-host pricing scaling nonlinearly with infrastructure growth, platform engineering team under budget pressure — leading to open-source observability as the solution category that addresses both cost and control concerns

To capitalize on the narrow evaluation window, the recommended first touch specifies LinkedIn as the channel (given Raj's active presence), recommends a peer-level tone that positions as sharing relevant perspective on the exact problem he is publicly discussing, sets the goal as entering the active evaluation, and flags that timing is the critical variable because the prospect has signaled he is already evaluating alternatives
